semantic_description: Verifies min_cost_two_steps, a scalar dynamic-programming computation over an input integer array cost of length n. For n = 1 it returns cost[0]; otherwise it initializes prev2 = cost[0] and prev1 = cost[0] + cost[1], then runs a for loop from i = 2 to n - 1 computing cur = min(prev1, prev2) + cost[i], shifting prev2 and prev1. The function returns the Coq specification min_cost_two_steps_spec(l) and preserves the input IntArray::full(cost, n, l). The loop may execute zero times when n = 2 and contains a branch on the order of prev1 and prev2.
